creator: olaf@zaplinski.de
text:
net.inet6.ip6.v6only is "1" by default:

# sysctl net.inet6.ip6.v6only
net.inet6.ip6.v6only: 1


I have adjusted the configuration:

# grep v6 lighttpd.conf
## Use IPv6?
server.use-ipv6 = "enable"
server.set-v6only = "enable"


The result:

# service lighttpd onestart
Starting lighttpd.

# netstat -an|grep 443
tcp4       0      0 *.443                  *.*                    
LISTEN

# netstat -an|grep 80
tcp4       0      0 *.80                   *.*                    
LISTEN
tcp6       0      0 2604:180:1::23bc.80    *.*                    
LISTEN

=> still no HTTPS on IPv4 and IPv6

I am giving up here. lighttpd is not light at all.
